reasoning: The forecast asks about a Russian crewed aircraft being shot down in NATO airspace within 6 months of a Sept 19, 2025 incident, giving a resolution date of March 19, 2026. The market probability was at 2% on March 6, 2026. Extensive web searches for the period spanning late 2025 through mid-March 2026 confirm that while drones have been shot down and there have been close calls where crewed jets were escorted out of airspace (like the initial Estonia incident), no Russian crewed aircraft has actually been shot down by NATO. With the knowledge cutoff at March 15, there are only 4 days left in the resolution window. Given the rarity of such events (only one in 2015 by Turkey) and the lack of any shootdown up to the cutoff date, I estimate the probability at exactly the 2% floor.
